title: نظام مكافحة غسل الأموال وتمويل الإرهاب
title_en: Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Law — Cyber Provisions
description: The Saudi Anti-Money Laundering Law governs the prevention, detection and prosecution of money laundering and terrorism financing, including through digital and cyber channels. The Law establishes obligations for financial institutions, virtual asset service providers, and other designated non-financial businesses and professions, and is enforced by the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) within the Saudi Central Bank (SAMA).

provisions.title: Article 1. Definitions Relevant to Cyber and Digital Activities
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law: 'Money Laundering' means any act or attempt to disguise the illegal origin of funds or property, or to convert, transfer, conceal or hide funds derived from criminal activity; 'Virtual Asset' means a digital representation of value that can be digitally traded or transferred, and can be used for payment or investment purposes, but does not include digital representations of fiat currencies, securities or other financial assets regulated under the Capital Market Law; 'Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP)' means any person who conducts, as a business, one or more activities involving virtual assets, including exchange, transfer, safekeeping, administration, or participation in initial coin offerings; 'Financial Institution' means any natural or legal person that conducts financial activities as a main business, including banks, insurance companies, exchange bureaux, payment service providers, and VASPs; 'FIU' means the Financial Intelligence Unit, the national centre responsible for receiving, analysing and disseminating suspicious transaction reports.

provisions.title: Article 2. Obligations of Financial Institutions — Customer Due Diligence
provisions.content: Financial institutions and VASPs shall implement customer due diligence (CDD) measures including: (1) identifying and verifying the identity of customers using reliable, independent source documents or electronic identity verification systems; (2) identifying and verifying the identity of beneficial owners of legal entities; (3) understanding and, as appropriate, obtaining information on the purpose and intended nature of the business relationship; (4) conducting ongoing monitoring of the business relationship, including scrutiny of transactions to ensure consistency with the institution's knowledge of the customer. Enhanced due diligence (EDD) shall be applied for: (1) high-risk customers, including politically exposed persons (PEPs); (2) customers from high-risk jurisdictions identified by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF); (3) transactions involving virtual assets above applicable thresholds; (4) remote or digital-only customer onboarding. Electronic and digital identity verification systems used for CDD shall comply with standards approved by SAMA.

provisions.title: Article 3. Cybercrime Proceeds and Digital Asset Tracing
provisions.content: The proceeds of cybercrimes, including ransomware payments, proceeds of online fraud, funds obtained through phishing, and gains from cryptojacking or other cyber-enabled financial crimes, shall constitute proceeds of crime subject to the provisions of this Law. Financial institutions and VASPs shall: (1) implement transaction monitoring systems capable of detecting patterns associated with cyber-enabled financial crimes, including unusual transactions following patterns typical of ransomware payments or money mule activity; (2) report suspicious transactions involving proceeds of cybercrime to the FIU without delay; (3) cooperate with SAMA and law enforcement in the tracing and freezing of proceeds of cybercrime, including virtual assets. VASPs shall implement blockchain analytics capabilities to identify transactions associated with illicit activity, sanctions evasion, or dark web activity. SAMA shall issue guidance on red flags indicating proceeds of cybercrime laundering through the financial system and through VASPs.

provisions.title: Article 4. Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs)
provisions.content: Virtual asset service providers operating in Saudi Arabia shall: (1) obtain a licence from SAMA before commencing operations; (2) implement the Travel Rule — when transferring virtual assets, collect and transmit to the beneficiary institution the originator information including name, account number and physical address; (3) implement enhanced transaction monitoring for virtual asset transactions; (4) not conduct business with unregistered or unregulated VASPs in other jurisdictions; (5) screen virtual asset transactions against sanctions lists maintained by SAMA; (6) implement CDD measures equivalent to those required of banks for customers with virtual asset holdings or transactions above prescribed thresholds; (7) report to the FIU any transaction where there are reasonable grounds to suspect money laundering, terrorist financing, or connection to cybercrime; (8) maintain records of all virtual asset transactions for at least 10 years. SAMA shall maintain a public register of licensed VASPs operating in Saudi Arabia.

provisions.title: Article 5. Cybersecurity Obligations for Financial Institutions
provisions.content: Financial institutions and VASPs shall implement cybersecurity measures to prevent their systems from being exploited for money laundering or terrorism financing: (1) implement fraud detection systems capable of identifying account takeover, identity theft, and synthetic identity fraud; (2) implement controls to detect and prevent the use of their systems as money mule infrastructure; (3) implement transaction velocity controls and unusual pattern detection; (4) implement cybersecurity measures consistent with the SAMA Cybersecurity Framework (SAMA CSF) and the NCA Essential Cybersecurity Controls; (5) conduct annual penetration testing and share results with SAMA; (6) implement multi-factor authentication for all digital banking and payment services; (7) report cybersecurity incidents that could facilitate money laundering to both SAMA and the FIU. Financial institutions shall maintain detailed audit logs of all digital financial transactions for a minimum of 10 years, and shall make these available to the FIU and law enforcement upon lawful request.

provisions.title: Article 6. Sanctions and Asset Freezing
provisions.content: The FIU and SAMA have the power to: (1) freeze assets of persons or entities under investigation for money laundering or terrorist financing, including virtual assets, pending judicial determination; (2) apply targeted financial sanctions in implementation of United Nations Security Council resolutions; (3) impose administrative sanctions on financial institutions and VASPs for violations of this Law. Administrative sanctions include: (1) written warning for minor violations; (2) financial penalty of up to SAR 5,000,000 for each violation; (3) suspension of licence for repeated violations; (4) revocation of licence for persistent or particularly serious violations; (5) publication of the violation and penalty. Criminal liability for money laundering: imprisonment of 5 to 15 years and a fine equal to twice the laundered amount. Criminal liability for operators facilitating money laundering through cyber means shall be the same, with the additional penalty of confiscation of all digital infrastructure used.

definitions.definition: Any act or attempt to disguise the illegal origin of funds or property, or to convert, transfer, conceal or hide funds derived from criminal activity.

A digital representation of value that can be digitally traded or transferred, and can be used for payment or investment purposes, but does not include digital representations of fiat currencies, securities or other financial assets.

definitions.term: مزود خدمة الأصول الافتراضية (Virtual Asset Service Provider / VASP)
definitions.definition: Any person who conducts, as a business, one or more activities involving virtual assets, including exchange, transfer, safekeeping, administration, or participation in initial coin offerings.
